## Summary

Wires the Phase Architecture v2 Review Gate handoff helper into `lib/evaluation/processor.ts` and updates public WAVE/Revise doctrine copy.

The processor no longer opens Review Gate from Story Layer readiness alone. It now uses `buildReviewGateHandoff(...)` so Review Gate opens only when:

- `pass1a_story_layer_v1` exists
- `ledger_quality_report_v1` exists
- Pass 3A is terminal-and-gate-valid

Public copy now separates WAVE as readiness governance from Revise as the author-controlled repair workflow.

## Scope

In scope:

- Wire `processEvaluationJob(...)` through `buildReviewGateHandoff(...)` before opening Review Gate.
- Preserve ready handoff semantics: `status=queued`, `phase=review_gate`, `phase_status=awaiting_approval`.
- Persist Phase v2 readiness fields: `review_gate_ready`, `pass3a_gate_validity`, `pass3a_status`, `pass3a_artifact_id`, and degraded reason where applicable.
- Block unsafe Review Gate handoff when Pass 3A is missing, running, half-written, failed, or degraded without structured proof.
- Persist safe blocked progress and requeue to `phase_1a`.
- Scrub public copy in `app/page.tsx` and `app/revise/page.tsx` so WAVE is not described as the revision workflow.

Out of scope:

- Scoring changes.
- Phase 2 criteria logic changes.
- Phase 3 synthesis changes.
- WAVE runtime changes.
- Quality Gate changes.
- Pass 3A extraction/provider changes.
- Manuscript chunking changes.
- Provider calls.
- Revise Queue runtime implementation changes.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

The branch was rebased onto current `origin/main` before the doctrine scrub commit.

## Risks & Anomalies

During PR hardening, Phase 3 test fixtures were updated so `processor.canonical-pipeline.test.ts` and `processor.real-gate.test.ts` provide the accepted Story Ledger governance rail now required by the Phase 3 gate.

This PR intentionally does not change Phase 3 scoring, synthesis, WAVE runtime behavior, provider behavior, or Quality Gate semantics.

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

Process Change: yes

- [x] Sequential phase-gate doctrine preserved (parallelism only within safe sub-workloads).
- [x] Phase 0 remains first and is proven before downstream processing.
- [x] Phase 2 remains blocked on accepted_story_ledger_v1 (Review Gate authority).
- [x] Phase 3 remains blocked on pass12_handoff_v1 and is sole owner of Pass 3B synthesis.
- [x] Deterministic quality gates run after Pass 3B and before completion.
- [x] WAVE remains post-evaluation (after evaluation_result_v2) and non-fatal to base evaluation.

Note: public-facing copy in this PR describes WAVE as evaluation/readiness governance and Revise as the repair workflow; the checked item above preserves the repository guard's current canonical wording.

One-line doctrine: The pipeline is sequential at the phase/gate level and parallel only inside safe sub-workloads.

Process-Change Impact Summary (required when Process Change: yes):
- Review Gate no longer opens from Story Layer readiness alone; it opens only when Story Layer, quality report, and Pass 3A terminal/gate-valid proof are present. Public copy now separates WAVE readiness governance from Revise repair execution.

## Contract Integrity

- Review Gate readiness is derived from artifacts and state, not hand-set.
- Blocked handoff does not persist non-canonical top-level `phase_status='blocked'`.
- Blocked diagnostics are persisted in progress as `block_code`, `block_reason`, `pass3a_status`, and `pass3a_gate_validity`.
- Existing Review Gate queued/awaiting approval semantics remain intact for valid handoff.
- Public UI copy no longer says WAVE is the repair workflow.

## Behavioral Quality

Expected behavior:

- Pass 3A `done` plus valid `pass3_preflight_draft_v1` opens Review Gate.
- Pass 3A `degraded` plus structured proof opens Review Gate.
- Missing/running/map_done/reduce_running/failed Pass 3A blocks Review Gate.
- Done-without-artifact blocks Review Gate.
- Degraded-without-proof blocks Review Gate.
- Website copy presents WAVE as readiness governance and Revise as author-controlled repair.

Quality Gate / anomaly disclosure: this PR does not change Quality Gate scoring or QG enforcement. It prevents premature Review Gate advancement and corrects public WAVE/Revise doctrine copy.

## Latency Evidence

This is a control-flow handoff and public-copy correction PR. It adds one bounded artifact lookup for three artifact types and deterministic readiness derivation. It does not add model calls, provider calls, manuscript reads, Pass 1/2/3 expansion, or WAVE runtime execution.

Baseline (Pre-change)

| Run | Scope | pass1_ms | pass2_ms | pass3_ms | total_ms | Notes |
| --- | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| Run 1 | Existing Phase 1A handoff | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A | No additional provider/model work measured. |
| Run 2 | Existing Phase 1A handoff | N/A | N/A | N/A | N/A | Baseline affected by unrelated existing real-gate test failure. |

Post-change Runs

| Run | Scope | pass1_ms | pass2_ms | pass3_ms | total_ms | Notes |
| --- | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- |
| Run 1 | Review Gate handoff helper tests | 0 | 0 | 0 | deterministic unit scope | No provider/model expansion. |
| Run 2 | Processor wiring helper tests | 0 | 0 | 0 | deterministic unit scope | Adds bounded artifact lookup only. |

Selected pass impact:

- [x] Pass 1
- [ ] Pass 2
- [ ] Pass 3

Latency metric: `pass1_ms` unchanged for model execution; `total_ms` impact is limited to one bounded `evaluation_artifacts` read and deterministic readiness derivation.

This is not reducing intelligence. The change preserves quality by adding deterministic proof requirements before Review Gate opens.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

N/A - this PR does not introduce new external inputs. It reads existing persisted `evaluation_artifacts` rows and existing job progress only.

## Internal Process Leakage

N/A - no private model reasoning is exposed. Only deterministic readiness status, block codes, artifact IDs, and public doctrine copy are changed.

## Input → Action → Output

Input: existing job progress, Review Gate artifact rows, and public page copy.

Action: derive Phase v2 Review Gate readiness through `buildReviewGateHandoff(...)` and correct public WAVE/Revise doctrine language.

Output: either safe Review Gate handoff or blocked progress with diagnostics and requeue to `phase_1a`; public pages distinguish WAVE readiness governance from Revise repair execution.

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

Public-safe metrics are limited to operational status fields such as `review_gate_ready`, `pass3a_gate_validity`, `pass3a_status`, `block_code`, and `block_reason`.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

No provider/model/runtime expansion. The change adds a bounded database artifact lookup and deterministic helper execution only. Public copy changes do not alter runtime behavior.

## Latency Impact

Expected latency impact is negligible relative to model execution. No additional provider calls are introduced. No prompt size, token budget, or WAVE runtime path is expanded.

## Tests Updated

- Added `__tests__/evaluation/reviewGateHandoff.phaseV2.test.ts`.
- Updated `__tests__/lib/evaluation/processor.test.ts` for helper derivation and artifact mapping.
- Updated `__tests__/lib/evaluation/processor.canonical-pipeline.test.ts` with accepted Story Ledger governance rail fixture coverage.
- Updated `__tests__/lib/evaluation/processor.real-gate.test.ts` with accepted Story Ledger governance rail fixture coverage.
- Updated architecture invariant coverage proving processor imports and calls the Phase Architecture v2 helper.

## No-Pipeline-Impact

N/A - this is an evaluation-pipeline control-flow PR, so pipeline impact is intentionally declared above rather than bypassed.
